req.title: Allergy intolerance (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-034 covers three distinct concern areas: (1) happy-path allergy creation and downstream conflict alert generation (EP/STC), (2) duplicate prevention via 409 (NEG/DT), and (3) boundary values on the recordedDate timestamp and numeric severity/criticality fields (BVA). Each test is self-contained by using a unique patient fixture per run and cleaning up via a teardown DELETE. The state-transition test additionally posts a MedicationRequest referencing the same substance to verify alert regeneration, exercising the STC arc from 'allergy-absent' to 'allergy-active' to 'conflict-detected'.

tests.name: test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token fixture>
No prior AllergyIntolerance for that patient/substance combination
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with valid payload referencing patient and substance penicillin
Assert HTTP 201 and Location header present
GET the returned Location URL
Assert resource round-trips with correct patient reference and clinicalStatus active
expected: Location header points to new resource
patient reference matches fixture patient ID
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path(auth_token, patient_id, base_url, cleanup_resource):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "recordedDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
    assert location, "Location header must be present"
    resource_id = location.rstrip("/").split("/")[-1].split("/_history")[0]
    cleanup_resource("AllergyIntolerance", resource_id)
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["clinicalStatus"]["coding"][0]["code"] == "active"
    assert patient_id in body["patient"]["reference"]

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token fixture>
An identical AllergyIntolerance (same patient + substance + recordedDate) already exists via <existing_allergy fixture>
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with payload identical to the pre-seeded resource (same patient, substance, recordedDate)
Assert HTTP 409 Conflict is returned
Assert response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code 'duplicate'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409(auth_token, patient_id, base_url, existing_allergy):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    duplicate_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "recordedDate": existing_allergy["recordedDate"]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=duplicate_payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", "Response must be OperationOutcome"
    codes = [issue.get("code") for issue in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any(c in ("duplicate", "conflict") for c in codes), f"No duplicate/conflict code found in {codes}"

tests.name: test_allergy_recorded_date_boundary_max_precision
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token fixture>
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with recordedDate at maximum FHIR dateTime precision including milliseconds and explicit UTC offset
Assert HTTP 201
GET resource and assert recordedDate is preserved or normalised without data loss
POST second AllergyIntolerance with recordedDate as date-only string (minimum precision boundary)
Assert HTTP 201 for date-only variant
expected: Stored recordedDate contains the same date portion '2024-12-31'
No 400/422 validation error for either boundary value

tests.name: test_medication_request_conflict_alert_after_allergy_creation
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token fixture>
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance for patient with penicillin allergy (state: allergy-absent -> allergy-active)
Assert HTTP 201
POST /MedicationRequest referencing same patient and penicillin medication (state: allergy-active -> conflict-detected)
Assert response contains detectedIssue or extension indicating allergy conflict warning
expected: MedicationRequest POST returns 201 or 200 with a conflict warning element
Response body includes detectedIssue reference or operationOutcome warning with code 'drug-allergy-alert' or equivalent
No silent suppression of the alert
trace: R-HLT-034: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_medication_request_conflict_alert_after_allergy_creation(auth_token, patient_id, base_url, cleanup_resource):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    allergy_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "recordedDate": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    }
    ar = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=allergy_payload, headers=headers)
    assert ar.status_code == 201, f"Allergy creation failed: {ar.status_code}"
    aid = ar.headers["Location"].rstrip("/").split("/")[-1].split("/_history")[0]
    cleanup_resource("AllergyIntolerance", aid)
    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
    }
    mr = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=med_payload, headers=headers)
    assert mr.status_code in (200, 201), f"MedicationRequest failed: {mr.status_code} {mr.text}"
    body = mr.json()
    has_alert = <check_conflict_alert>(body)
    assert has_alert, f"Expected allergy conflict alert in response but none found: {body}"

tests.name: test_allergy_numeric_criticality_boundary_values
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token fixture>
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with criticality set to 'low' (minimum valid FHIR value)
Assert HTTP 201
POST /AllergyIntolerance with criticality set to 'high' (maximum valid FHIR value)
Assert HTTP 201
POST /AllergyIntolerance with criticality set to 'INVALID_VALUE' (out-of-bound value)
Assert HTTP 400 or 422 with OperationOutcome
trace: R-HLT-034: Boundary cases on numeric elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_allergy_numeric_criticality_boundary_values(auth_token, patient_id, base_url, cleanup_resource):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    def build(criticality):
        return {
            "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
            "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
            "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
            "criticality": criticality,
            "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "1191", "display": "Aspirin"}]},
            "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
            "recordedDate": "2024-03-01T00:00:00Z"
        }
    for crit in ("low", "high"):
        r = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=build(crit), headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 201, f"criticality={crit} rejected: {r.status_code} {r.text}"
        rid = r.headers["Location"].rstrip("/").split("/")[-1].split("/_history")[0]
        cleanup_resource("AllergyIntolerance", rid)
    bad = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=build("INVALID_VALUE"), headers=headers)
    assert bad.status_code in (400, 422), f"Expected 400/422 for invalid criticality, got {bad.status_code}"
    assert bad.json().get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
